tool.name: aoptk parse PDF

configfiles.configfile:
import os import sys
import yaml from aoptk.literature.pdf import PDF from aoptk.literature.pymupdf_parser import PymupdfParser from aoptk.text_generation_api import TextGenerationAPI
#if $input_source.input_mode == "single" pdf_inputs = [("$input_source.input_file", "$input_source.input_file.element_identifier")] #else pdf_inputs = [ #for $pdf in $input_source.input_collection ("$pdf", "$pdf.element_identifier"), #end for ] #end if
#if $use_llms == "true" @MODEL_SETUP@ text_generation=TextGenerationAPI(model=model, api_key=litellm_api_key, url=litellm_base_url)
#else text_generation = None #end if
for input_path, element_identifier in pdf_inputs: publication_id = os.path.splitext(element_identifier)[0] text = PymupdfParser([PDF(input_path)], text_generation=text_generation).get_publications(download_figures_enabled=False)[0].full_text with open(f"{publication_id}.txt", "w") as f: f.write(text)

param.name: input_mode
param.label: Input type
param.help: Choose whether to parse one PDF or every PDF in a collection.

param.name: use_llms
param.label: Use LLMs to parse scanned PDFs
param.help: Check if you want to use LLMs to parse old PDFs which contain scans of text as images and not text encoded through OCR.

tool.help:
Parse PDF ===================
Tool to parse PDF files.
